violations.text: Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (soup) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025
Beef, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview with PIC, beef was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Reviewed cooling methods with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025

1) Raw animal foods (shrimp) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables, dressings) in the walk-in refrigerator. 2) Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (peppers, onions) in the prep unit. 3) Raw animal foods (raw beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cooked beef) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

Beef, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

Cheese was observed held at 48-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector informed PIC to not stack cheese in prep unit. Cheese was moved to another cooler at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

(Stainless Steel Cleaner) was stored on prep unit shelf. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live cockroach in drive thru area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

Knives were stored on an unclean surface (in between hot/cold service line) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025
Single service items were observed stored on the floor in the outside storage container. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025
Missing floor tiles were observed in the following areas: 1) Walk-in cooler 2) Prep area The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Prep sink was lacking a proper air gap. An air gap is required in order to prevent the backflow of solid, liquid, or gaseous contaminants into the potable water supply at each point of use within a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
